---
title: "A Reproducible Scientific Manuscript"
subtitle: "One source → HTML, PDF (LaTeX) and Word"
author:
  - name: "Dr Abdul Mannan"
    affiliations:
      - name: "Bangor Haemophilia Centre, BCUHB"
abstract: |
  This template shows a journal-style manuscript authored once in Quarto and
  rendered to HTML, PDF and DOCX. It demonstrates executable figures, numbered
  tables, cross-references and citations from a shared BibTeX file.
keywords: [quarto, reproducibility, haematology]
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    number-sections: true
  docx:
    number-sections: true
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

## Introduction

Reproducible reporting keeps the analysis and the narrative in one place.
Emicizumab prophylaxis, for example, was established by landmark trial evidence
[@mahlangu2018haven3]. Methods conventions follow @example2025method.

## Methods

We simulate a one-compartment pharmacokinetic model (@fig-decay) and tabulate
concentrations at fixed times (@tbl-conc).

```{python}
#| label: fig-decay
#| fig-cap: "Simulated plasma concentration after a 1000 mg IV bolus."
#| echo: false
import numpy as np, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
ke = np.log(2)/12; t = np.linspace(0,48,300); C = (1000/50)*np.exp(-ke*t)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6,3.6))
ax.plot(t, C, color="#B71C1C", lw=2); ax.set_xlabel("Time (h)"); ax.set_ylabel("mg/L")
ax.grid(alpha=.3); plt.show()
```

## Results

```{python}
#| label: tbl-conc
#| tbl-cap: "Concentration at selected times."
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
ts=[0,6,12,24,48]
pd.DataFrame({"Time (h)":ts,"Conc (mg/L)":[round((1000/50)*np.exp(-ke*x),2) for x in ts]})
```

As shown in @fig-decay and @tbl-conc, concentration halves every 12 hours.

## Discussion

The same `.qmd` produced this HTML page, a typeset PDF and an editable Word file
with no manual re-formatting.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
